title: Author canonical pheno-zod-schemas + pheno-pydantic-models dual-stack schemas
author: forge

downstream: L5-#81..85 (event-pipeline serialization, FastAPI request/response models, Node/Hono RPC handlers)

summary: Authored two tightly-paired dual-stack schema packages: pheno-zod-schemas (TypeScript + Zod 3.23, ESM, strict TS 5.4) and pheno-pydantic-models (Python >=3.11 + Pydantic 2.6 + email-validator). Both packages mirror the same three-entity domain (User, WorklogEntry, Project) with identical field names, identical constraint semantics (e.g. min(1).max(80) on display_name mirrors Field(min_length=1, max_length=80)), and identical 6-value WorklogStatus enum (pending, running, blocked, completed, failed, cancelled). Each package exports its own runtime validators + inferred types, plus 5 mirrored tests. Tripwire assertion in both suites pins the WorklogStatus cardinality at exactly 6 to catch accidental wire-contract drift.

pheno-zod-schemas.WorklogEntrySchema: z.ZodObject - {task_id, status:enum, agent_id, commit_sha:string|null, started_at, completed_at, files_changed:array(string)}
pheno-zod-schemas.WorklogStatus: readonly tuple of 6 canonical status strings
pheno-zod-schemas.ProjectSchema: z.ZodObject + .refine - {id:slug, name(1..120), owner_email, members:array(email)>=1} with 'owner must be a member' invariant

pheno-pydantic-models.User: pydantic v2 BaseModel - {id:UUID4, email:EmailStr, display_name:str(1..80), created_at:AwareDatetime} with model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid', frozen=True)
pheno-pydantic-models.WorklogEntry: pydantic v2 BaseModel - {task_id:str, status:WorklogStatus, agent_id:str, commit_sha:str|None, started_at:AwareDatetime, completed_at:AwareDatetime|None, files_changed:list[str]} with model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid', frozen=True) and a 7-40 lowercase hex commit_sha regex
pheno-pydantic-models.WorklogStatus: str Enum of 6 canonical status strings (pending, running, blocked, completed, failed, cancelled)
pheno-pydantic-models.Project: pydantic v2 BaseModel - {id:slug-regex, name:str(1..120), owner_email:EmailStr, members:list[EmailStr]>=1} with model_validator(mode='after') enforcing 'owner must be a member'
pheno-pydantic-models.COMMIT_SHA_PATTERN: re.Pattern - 7-40 lowercase hex (exported so consumers can reuse)
pheno-pydantic-models.SLUG_PATTERN: re.Pattern - lowercase alnum with - or _ (exported so consumers can reuse)

zod_tsc_typecheck.result: clean (no errors, strict:true / target:ES2022 / module:NodeNext / moduleResolution:NodeNext)

test_coverage: user_schema_rejects_invalid_email (TS + Python) - email validator catches malformed input on both sides
worklog_entry_schema_accepts_all_six_statuses (TS + Python) - iterates over the 6 canonical WorklogStatus enum values and asserts each validates
worklog_entry_schema_rejects_unknown_status (TS + Python) - a status string outside the 6-member enum is rejected
project_schema_requires_at_least_one_owner_member (TS + Python) - empty members list rejected, owner not in members rejected, well-formed fixture passes
schema_types_infer_correctly (TS + Python) - round-trip parse/construct proves the inferred types match the runtime validators, plus the 6-status tripwire pins the cardinality
user_schema_accepts_well_formed_user (TS only) - baseline accept test so the reject tests are meaningful (analogous test implicit in Python suite)
idempotency: Both packages are pure data-validation libraries. Schemas are stateless (no global state, no side effects on parse). Calling .parse() / Model() multiple times with identical input is fully deterministic. Frozen=True on the Python models means instances are immutable values; the TS schemas do not have a frozen equivalent (objects are plain JS values), but the package itself never mutates the input it validates.
consolidation_targets: phenotype-zod-schemas (older ad-hoc Zod schema dump in the monorepo - now superseded by the canonical pheno-zod-schemas)
phenotype-python-sdk (which carried inline Pydantic models for some entities - these can now be re-exported from pheno-pydantic-models)
downstream_consumers: L3-#58 (pheno-bus) - serializes WorklogEntry over the message bus; will use these schemas as the wire contract
L5-#81..85 (event-pipeline services) - import either package depending on the service's stack
pheno-fastapi-base (L3 #51) - imports pydantic models for FastAPI request/response bodies
Agentora, Conft, AuthKit, and other pheno-* services - can consume the zod schemas for Hono/Express handlers

verification.zod_vitest: 6/6 pass in 256ms (vitest 1.6.1, zod 3.25.76, typescript 5.9.3, node v25.9.0)
verification.zod_tsc: tsc --noEmit clean (no type errors with strict:true)
verification.pydantic_pytest: 5/5 pass (.venv with pydantic 2.13.4, pydantic-core 2.46.4, email-validator 2.3.0, pytest 9.0.3, python 3.14.5)
verification.system_pip_status: PEP 668-blocked on the host; venv used to install pydantic into an isolated environment. The README documents the venv pattern for downstream consumers on similar hosts.

notes: Authored as a single commit on chore/l3-53-pheno-zod-pydantic-2026-06-11 off main 52dfc7aa06 (fdf6b10706 feat(pheno-zod-schemas,pheno-pydantic-models): author canonical dual-stack schemas (L3 #53)). Branch is local-only per task directive (NOT pushed). Field-by-field parity (TS zod <-> Python pydantic v2): User.id is z.string().uuid() vs UUID4 (canonical, not bare UUID, to reject v1/v3/v5); User.email is z.string().email() vs EmailStr; User.display_name is z.string().min(1).max(80) vs Annotated[str, Field(min_length=1, max_length=80)]; User.created_at is ISO-8601 string with offset validation vs AwareDatetime (rejects naive); WorklogEntry.status is z.enum(WorklogStatus) vs WorklogStatus StrEnum of the same 6 strings; WorklogEntry.commit_sha is z.string().nullable() with 7-40 lowercase hex regex vs Optional[str] with the same regex enforced via field_validator; Project.id is slug regex vs Field(pattern=slug regex); Project.name is z.string().min(1).max(120) vs Annotated[str, Field(min_length=1, max_length=120)]; Project.owner_email is z.string().email() vs EmailStr; Project.members is z.array(z.string().email()).min(1) vs Annotated[list[EmailStr], Field(min_length=1)] plus a model_validator(mode='after') for the owner-must-be-member invariant (the TS side uses .refine on the schema for the same invariant). The tripwire assertion in both test suites pins the WorklogStatus cardinality at exactly 6, mirroring the l3-46 (pheno-errors) 6-variant tripwire pattern in the Rust sibling crate. Adding a 7th status value is a breaking change to the wire contract and must be coordinated across both packages.
no_touch: Other L3 tasks (L3-#46 pheno-errors, L3-#47 pheno-tracing, L3-#48 pheno-config, L3-#49 pheno-otel, L3-#50 pheno-cli-base, L3-#51 pheno-fastapi-base, L3-#52 pheno-go-ctxkit, L3-#54 pheno-tower-stack, L3-#55 pheno-ssot-template, L3-#56 pheno-flags, L3-#57 pheno-plugin-registry) - all untouched
Root Cargo.toml [workspace.members] - pheno-zod-schemas and pheno-pydantic-models are standalone packages, not workspace members
Root package.json / .gitignore - left in place; the new packages are net-new at the monorepo root

phenotype-zod-schemas (older ad-hoc Zod dump) and phenotype-python-sdk (which carried inline Pydantic models) - left in place for now; the new pheno-zod-schemas and pheno-pydantic-models are the canonical paths for new code
